title: C Memory Outcomes, Evidence, and Toolchain Boundaries · C Contract Brief: Translation Execution Model
summary: A civic sensor team must distinguish a passing browser model from evidence that its native C release is safe on supported targets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem translation execution model increment in memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace source, preprocessing, translation, linking, loading, execution, and observable output without collapsing their failure layers.
Pin ISO/IEC 9899:2024, reviewed implementation support, diagnostics, extensions, and target ABI as a compatibility contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Translation Execution Model · Theory Translation Execution Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8333. Produce bounded C evidence to separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Translation Execution Model contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Translation Execution Model evidence · Theory Translation Execution Model
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-translation-execution-model, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-translation-execution-model-146q1f */" for changed case 5178. It must separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
